mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 4.5% signals strong labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE < 4.5%, or the correlation between UNRATE and SPX returns falls below 0.25.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in housing starts above 2.0 million units signals robust demand for residential construction, increasing corporate revenues and equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in 60%+ of instances where HOUST > 2000, or the predictive power of HOUST declines below 0.15 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation (CPIAUCSL > 2.5%) combined with low interest rates (FEDFUNDS < 3.5%) signals central bank inaction, creating uncertainty that depresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL > 2.5% and FEDFUNDS < 3.5%, or the interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS loses statistical significance (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals a tightening cycle that raises borrowing costs and reduces corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in 60%+ of instances where FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, or the predictive power of FEDFUNDS declines below 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in industrial production above 105 signals strong manufacturing activity that increases corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in 60%+ of instances where INDPRO > 105, or the correlation between INDPRO and SPX returns falls below 0.20.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in real sales (RSAFS > 350,000 million) signals strong consumer demand that boosts corporate revenues, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in 60%+ of instances where RSAFS > 350000, or the predictive power of RSAFS declines below 0.15 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS > 130,000) combined with low unemployment (UNRATE < 5%) signals a tight labor market that increases wage pressures and corporate costs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in 60%+ of instances where PAYEMS > 130000 and UNRATE < 5%, or the interaction term between PAYEMS and UNRATE loses statistical significance (p > 0.10).
